name: machine-rtc
description: Real-Time Clock for date/time management. Survives deep sleep on ESP32 (powered by RTC domain). Also provides RTC memory for data preservation across sleep.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific RTC interface. Epoch is 2000-01-01, not 1970.

classes.name: RTC

methods.name: RTC.datetime

parameters.name: datetime_tuple
parameters.description: Optional 8-tuple: (year, month, day, weekday, hours, minutes, seconds, subseconds). weekday=0-6 (Mon=0). subseconds=microseconds. None to query.

returns.description: If reading: (year, month, day, weekday, hours, minutes, seconds, subseconds).

methods.description: Get or set RTC datetime. Weekday is auto-calculated when setting.

methods.name: RTC.memory

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Data to store in RTC memory. None to read current data.

methods.description: Get or set RTC memory. Data persists through deep sleep.

classes.example:
from machine import RTC

rtc = RTC()

# Set time: (year, month, day, weekday, hour, min, sec, subsec)
rtc.datetime((2024, 6, 15, 5, 10, 30, 0, 0))

# Read current time
dt = rtc.datetime()
print(f'{dt[0]}-{dt[1]:02d}-{dt[2]:02d} {dt[4]:02d}:{dt[5]:02d}:{dt[6]:02d}')

# Store data in RTC memory (survives deep sleep)
rtc.memory(b'wake_count:5')
print(rtc.memory())  # b'wake_count:5'

esp32_notes: ESP32 RTC survives deep sleep (powered by RTC domain).
RTC.memory() returns up to 4096 bytes on ESP32 (8KB total, half reserved).
Weekday is calculated automatically when setting datetime.
Microsecond subsecond field is typically 0 on ESP32 (no sub-second resolution).
